name: Brute Force (every pair)
summary: Try every buy day i paired with every later sell day j, compute the profit, and keep the largest — the O(n²) baseline the one-pass approach improves on.
visualizationIntent: init: prices shown as a row of day-cells; max_profit starts at 0. loop: pointer i fixes the buy day (special). move: pointer j sweeps every later sell day (current). check: profit = prices[j] - prices[i]; a readout shows this trial profit against the best. update: whenever the trial profit beats max_profit, the best jumps (key event). return: the result panel holds the max profit found across all pairs.

lineExplanations.3: Start the best profit at 0 — if no pair is profitable, the answer stays 0.
lineExplanations.4: Record the number of days so both loops know where to stop.
lineExplanations.5: Outer loop: fix a day i to buy on.
lineExplanations.6: Inner loop: try selling on each later day j (must be after the buy day).
lineExplanations.7: Compute the profit of buying on day i and selling on day j.
lineExplanations.8: Check whether this pair's profit beats the best found so far.
lineExplanations.9: It does — record this pair's profit as the new best.
lineExplanations.10: Every pair has been tried — return the largest profit found.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def maxProfit(self, prices): — defines the method receiving the list of daily prices.
syntaxExplanations.3: max_profit = 0 — best profit so far; 0 means no profitable trade.
syntaxExplanations.4: n = len(prices) — the number of days, used to bound both loops.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): — outer loop picks the buy day i.
syntaxExplanations.6: for j in range(i + 1, n): — inner loop picks a sell day j strictly after i.
syntaxExplanations.7: profit = prices[j] - prices[i] — profit of buying on day i, selling on day j.
syntaxExplanations.8: if profit > max_profit: — is this pair better than the best so far?
syntaxExplanations.9: max_profit = profit — record the new best profit.
syntaxExplanations.10: return max_profit — the maximum profit over all pairs.
